content: Anyone have any idea how easy/hard/impossible it will be to get TikZ working, or more generally fiddling with our LaTeX preamble?
the LaTeX renderer Zulip uses does not support TikZ and although there is an open issue for xy support, no-one is currently working on it
so the best options for now are to paste images
thanks!
if there was a server-side LaTeX renderer, we could possibly integrate a bot that would respond to TikZ code, but I'm not aware of one existing
Any hackers with lots of spare time on their hands could try to integrate LaTeXit, an existing LaTeX-to-svg renderer. (Or with lots and lots of time, TikZit.......)
I guess you have to take the telegram bot that converts latex to images and posts them for you, and adapt it to zulip
I don't know how hard that is.

if we get this to work it'll be great. with 180 members, we already have more than enough know-how to do it.
but for some reason, only a small subset are subscribed to the meta stream.
Well, consider that knowing how to do something != doing it. It could still take many hours to implement such a thing, and I am fairly convinced many people wouldn't like to spend their time that way

At some point we should try to port the telegram TikZ bot to this, cos that would be a total lifesaver. We'll have to see how different the APIs are. This could be a nice weekend project, time permitting.
Yeah, that would be huge. We can then find a way to support tikzcd as well.
Scratch that, it is quite diffucult
Telegram supports inline bots, while I don't think that Zulip does
that's a big difference
I don't know how zulip renders Latex so I don't have an opinion on the difficulty right now :/.
Zulip uses KaTeX to render LaTeX
meaning if you want Zulip to support TikZ natively, you'll need to add it to KaTeX, which is not a small undertaking...
The idea wasn't fiddling with KaTeX

The idea was to deploy a bot. You address the bot in your message together with the latex you wanna render, the bot renders it and drops the png in the chat. At that point KaTeX is not used, the bot can run a full TeXLive under the hood

That's what InLaTeX bot does on Telegram. But that's an inline bot, meaning that as soon as you type @inlatex and start writing code you have a popup preview window, and you can press send only when everything looks ok.
(Which is actually pretty damn neat if you ask me)
That's a good idea! But it doesn't have to be server-side TexLive, I guess there's some online tool we can use, i.e. the bot issuing a web request to get back rendered data.
Especially while typesetting diagrams errors are frequent, so having a preview is very cool and avoids flooding the chat with wrong code
So I initially thought "cool, let's just port the inline telegram bot to here, the source is public"
But then again, zulip does not support inlne bots to my knowledge, making everything difficult

Also, I understand that adding tikz to KaTeX is very hard, but it's also incredible that on KaTeX' Github there is only one issue about this, and it has been insta-closed with a wontfix in 2015. It would be about time to do something about it
@Fabrizio Genovese: there's actually an open issue about commutative diagrams specifically (https://github.com/KaTeX/KaTeX/issues/1834)
though that only covers some of the use-cases

it has been insta-closed with a wontfix in 2015

I think that's because it's a monumental task
Yes, I was that issue, but it's really not the same. Practically everyone in this community relies on tikz, one way or another. I think KaTeX is just not enough for us.
if someone came along with a pull request, I don't think they'd be quick to turn it down
I agree having a TikZ bot would be ideal

Fabrizio Genovese said:

But then again, zulip does not support inlne bots to my knowledge, making everything difficult

Wait, do you mean that one can't explicitly invoke a bot?
as in: the bot will only respond to sent messages
No, I mean that when you type @inlinebot on telegram the dialogue with the bot is interactive even before sending a message
Exactly what @Nathanael Arkor says
Well, then the bot can react to a message by modifying it, right?
Well, yes and no

for instance I can say @bot a \to b \Sum c. This is wrong, cos Sum is capitalized, so the bot won't display anything. So I send another message @bot a \to b \sum c. The bot replies with a correctly rendered pictures, but my two messages stay in the main chat window
Now imagine that these messages aren't just a line, but 4000 chars each
You get the chat flooded with garbage code really quickly
An obvious workaround would be to address the bot in a private chat specifying topic and stream to publish to, but that becomes really cumbersome quite quickly
(moved the messages to the relevant thread)

Fabrizio Genovese said:

for instance I can say @bot a \to b \Sum c. This is wrong, cos Sum is capitalized, so the bot won't display anything. So I send another message @bot a \to b \sum c. The bot replies with a correctly rendered pictures, but my two messages stay in the main chat window

Modifying a message does not re-activate bots?
@Stelios Tsampas: that doesn't seem clear from the documentation; we'd probably have to ask in the Zulip server
I guess at worst we would have to make a "on-modify" bot? Anyway I should stop making conjectures on stuff I don't know :P.

Testing pasting directly from TikZiT:
image.png

That seems to work pretty well. If you preview a picture in TikZiT, you can copy it to clipboard by right clicking on it:
Screenshot-from-2020-04-28-11-24-56.png
and paste into Zulip. Not as smooth as you might like (and no tikz source saved), but a pretty quick solution.
